---
title: "Notation and Symbols"
---

## Purpose

This appendix collects the shorthand used across the book so readers do not have to infer the meaning of the same terms from context alone.

## Common notation

- `frame` or sampling frame: the set of units that could have been sampled
- `stratum`: a design group used for sampling and reporting
- `count`: an observed count of anglers, boats, or similar units
- `interview`: a trip, party, or completed interaction with anglers
- `weight`: the factor that expands a sampled unit to the target domain
- `estimate`: the expanded quantity reported for the population

## Unit reminders

When reading the book, keep an eye on the unit that a number refers to. The same symbol may not mean the same thing in every chapter if the estimand changes.

## Reading rule

If a formula, table, or code example seems ambiguous, check the chapter’s purpose and the “Key ideas” section first. The book is written to keep the design and the estimator aligned.
